one_line_thesis: Japan's dominant exchange and clearing infrastructure — a Stahl croupier with ~58% operating margins and volume leverage — but earnings are cyclical with market turnover; do not treat FY2025 peak activity as a permanent baseline.

deep_dive.executive_summary: Japan Exchange Group is Japan's exchange and clearing toll booth with ~58% operating margins and stable moat characteristics. FY2025 was a volume spike year: revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), net income ¥79.1B, ROE 23.1%. We normalize owner earnings to ¥70/share, below peak EPS ¥76.81, to adjust for clearing pass-through and cyclical turnover. At ¥2,190 (2026-07-21), total synthesis return 3.2% per year over 7 years, well below the 15% hold band. Lawrence scenario envelope: bear -6.3%, base 1.7%, bull 5.8%. Universal contract component base value ¥1,800/share vs spot. We propose watch at cycle-peak...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

change_control.rule: A source fact is locked. Every assumption change requires a reason, author, timestamp, and before/after value.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Complete isolated rater outputs, proposer case, pre-mortem, and research response without sharing peer votes.

method_fit.label: Predictable cash-flow security
applicability_tests: Incremental capital can be tied to identifiable projects, cohorts, or contracted capacity.
Returns on new capital can be separated from returns on the installed base.
Contract duration, volume protection, pricing, and counterparty risk are observable.
Leverage and future owner-funded capital are explicitly deducted.
failure_modes: contract renewal or regulatory reset ignored

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

economic_value.accounting_reference: Existing research and filing-derived valuation inputs. The primary-source reconciliation remains an open evidence gate.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of 8697.T, including every currently identified operating claim, financial claim, liability, reserve, and material option.
economic_claim.unit_source: Derived from inputs.shares_millions in the pre-existing valuation.json; [HUMAN REVIEW] confirm against the latest primary filing.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Operating and asset claims are valued once; cash, debt, financing, reserves, and other senior claims are separate components or explicitly reserved. No component is intended to overlap another.

valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from 8697.T/02_Quarterly/Earnings_Releases/E_ER_JPX_Q4FY2025.pdf and 8697.T/02_Quarterly/Explanatory_Materials/E_EM_JPX_Q4FY2025.pdf; component schedule reconciled 2026-07-21 contract backfill.
valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.
valuation_proof.overlap_control: Unique overlap key core_engine; no other component capitalizes the same claim.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows that the economic claim, cash conversion, ownership, or required capital is materially worse than this low case.

valuation_proof.adjustment: The low/base/high range incorporates ownership, tax, capital, cycle, timing, and realization uncertainty pending the primary bridge.

timeline.label: FY2025 peak: revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), net income ¥79.1B, cash equities ADV ¥7.52T (+31.9% YoY)
FY2024 operating revenue ¥162.2B; parent equity ~¥345B context in annual report
TSE reform program accelerates corporate governance and inbound equity engagement narrative
Tokyo Stock Exchange Group merges with Osaka Securities Exchange (66.67% acquired)
JPX listing era begins as consolidated exchange group post-merger integration

industry.structure: JPX holds a regulatory and network monopoly on Japanese cash equities, derivatives clearing, and listing infrastructure. Fee pools scale with turnover and collateral balances; operating margins exceed 50% at cycle peaks.
industry.share_shift: Domestic retail and inbound equity flows drove FY2025 ADV +31.9% YoY; global exchange peers (CME, ICE, Deutsche Börse) compete for derivatives and data but JPX remains domestically dominant.
industry.trend: TSE governance reform and NISA-era retail participation support structural engagement, but FY2027 management guidance implies flat-to-down operating income versus the FY2025 spike.

top_claims.claim: Japan Exchange Group is Japan's exchange and clearing toll booth with ~58% operating margins and stable moat characteristics. FY2025 was a volume spike year: revenue ¥198.7B (+22.5%), net income ¥79.1B, ROE 23.1%. We normalize owner earnings to ¥70/share, below peak EPS ¥76.81, to adjust for clearing pass-through and c
We expect **3.19%** per year over 7 years at **~$2000.5** on **$70/sh** starting owner cash.
*Primary risk: Prolonged slump in Japanese equity turnover after the FY2025 retail/inbound boom, compressing trading fees and clearing collateral income.
Clearing collateral income normalization as rates and volumes change.

risk_claims.claim: *Primary risk: Prolonged slump in Japanese equity turnover after the FY2025 retail/inbound boom, compressing trading fees and clearing collateral income.
Clearing collateral income normalization as rates and volumes change.
Regulatory fee caps on exchange services.
